page: Google Cloud Platform n4-highmem-64 is a memory-optimized server instance featuring 64 vCPUs and 512 GB of RAM, yielding an 8.0 GB RAM-to-vCPU ratio. Powered by an Intel x86_64 processor with 32 physical cores and 2 threads per core, this dedicated instance includes 260 MB of L3 cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 48 KB of L1d cache. It does not include bundled local storage or GPUs, and provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong memory and computational capabilities. The instance achieved a Passmark CPU score of 62,986.78 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 17,904.0, both placing in the top 25% tier. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with read-only large block speeds reaching 276.11 GB/sec and write large block speeds at 121.94 GB/sec. Advanced vector and matrix extensions, including AVX-512 and Intel AMX (Advanced Matrix Extensions), support accelerated workloads, as demonstrated by strong performance in image processing and medium-model LLM inference.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the n4-highmem-64 offers a dense memory-to-vCPU allocation suitable for memory-bound workloads without the overhead of dedicated GPU hardware. The high memory bandwidth and strong database operations score of 29,375.76 on Passmark make it highly suitable for in-memory databases, Redis deployments achieving 70.51 million SET operations per second, large-scale web serving, and software compilation.
description: Google Cloud Platform n4-highmem-64 is a memory-optimized server configured with 64 vCPUs and 512 GB of RAM, running on a dedicated Intel x86_64 architecture with 32 physical cores. Lacking local storage and GPU accelerators, this instance focuses its resource density on high-capacity memory tasks, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. Benchmark results show strong capabilities, including a Passmark CPU score of 62,986.78 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 17,904.0. It excels in memory bandwidth, reaching 276.11 GB/sec for large-block reads, and demonstrates high throughput in database operations and Redis SET operations. Supported by Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and AVX-512, the server is well-suited for memory-intensive workloads, including in-memory databases, large-scale web serving, and medium-scale LLM inference.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-highmem-64 is an x86_64 server with 64 vCPUs and 512 GB RAM. It features strong memory bandwidth and Intel AMX support, supporting in-memory databases and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-highmem-64 is an x86_64 server with 64 vCPUs and 512 GB RAM, optimized for memory-intensive database and web serving workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized x86_64 server featuring sixty-four vCPUs, half a terabyte of RAM, and advanced matrix extensions for memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 64 vCPUs and 512 GB RAM on a dedicated Intel x86_64 architecture
Strong memory bandwidth with 276.11 GB/sec large-block read speeds
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated vector and matrix operations
High-throughput database performance, executing 70.51 million Redis SET operations per second
Optimized for in-memory databases, large-scale web serving, and software compilation
